evidence_quotes.quote: Which, on the other hand, is a dark pattern of creating the said games in advance in order to force people to buy the consecutive titles

paragraphs.text: Hey everyone i pray everything is going magnificent with you! I wanted to let everyone reading this k ow about the Stop Killing Games initiative, aiming to make it possible for anyone who paid a full-priced game, to retain it and all content of the said game completely operational. Many different companies make games pre-released on the markets as a "Service" or Online-Only. Meaning that, at some given point in the future, the game would become completely unplayable for the owners, as there is nothing presented that can help retain and keep the game running and accessible without the need for a server-side connection. Which, on the other hand, is a dark pattern of creating the said games in advance in order to force people to buy the consecutive titles in order to keep playing and be part of the franchise. Which also involves greed. That said, i suggest that we change this immediately (as soon as possible), so that we can prevent the future abuse of the owners' rights of retaining their product and the complete functionality of it, which was fully paid for as it is not a license for it, but ownership to be precise. Thank you for the attention and have a blessed rest of the week :)
